tools.name: auth
tools.description:
Authenticate the MCP session with Quadratic.

Actions:
• login() — Start an OAuth device authorization flow. Returns a URL the user must open in a browser to authorize. The flow is completed by confirm_login.
• confirm_login(device_code) — Complete an in-progress login by polling for user authorizat

tools.name: files_read
tools.description:
Read-only file metadata operations on Quadratic files the user has access to. No data is modified. Requires authentication.

Actions:
• list_files() — List all accessible files.
• get_file_info(file_id) — Get metadata for a single file.

tools.name: files_write
tools.description:
File management operations that create or modify state: create a new file, open an existing file to start an editing session, or close a session. Requires authentication.

Actions:
• open_file(file_id?, file_name?) — Open a file by UUID or name and start an editing session. Returns the file's web UR

tools.name: read_data
tools.description:
Read-only queries on the open spreadsheet. No data is modified. Safe to auto-approve. Call as {"action": "<name>", "params": {...}} — per-action params are listed in the Action Reference below.

tools.name: write_data
tools.description:
Write operations on the open spreadsheet. Call as {"action": "<name>", "params": {...}} — per-action params are listed in the Action Reference below. Numbers, booleans, and nulls in cell values are coerced to strings.

Special actions (not shown in the action enum):
• batch — {"action": "batch", "pa

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "auth" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Authenticate the MCP session with Quadratic. Actions: • login() — Start an OAuth device authorization flow. Returns a URL the user must open in a browser to aut…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "read_data" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "read_data" is 5074 chars, far longer than this server's median (897). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Read-only queries on the open spreadsheet. No data is modified. Safe to auto-approve. Call as {"action": "<name>", "params": {...}} — per-action params are list…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "write_data" is 9055 chars, far longer than this server's median (897). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Write operations on the open spreadsheet. Call as {"action": "<name>", "params": {...}} — per-action params are listed in the Action Reference below. Numbers, b…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "write_data" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Write operations on the open spreadsheet. Call as {"action": "<name>", "params": {...}} — per-action params are listed in the Action Reference below. Numbers, b…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: auth, files_write, read_data · Private data: auth, files_read, files_write, read_data, write_data · Public sink: auth, read_data · Destructive: files_write, write_data.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: auth, files_write, read_data · Private data: auth, files_read, files_write, read_data, write_data · Public sink: auth, read_data · Destructive: files_write, write_data

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
